title: mentha arvensis var. subcordata
intro: Mentha arvensis var. subcordata, also known as wild mint, is a variant of the peppermint plant that is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis var. subcordata include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues, such as headaches, nausea, and colds. Wild mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, stress, and anxiety.
